topics: 🧑‍⚖️ R' Ze'ira: a zomem witness disqualifies himself, not by court ruling
📅 R' Yose: one testimony with one warning — partial nullification = total nullification
🗓️ The Nisan timeline: ten days of testimony retroactively void
🔁 Witnesses for theft and slaughter arriving separately — and being 'reactivated'
📜 New Mishnah: עד אחד or עצמו for slaughter ⇒ only כפל, not ד׳ וה׳

overview.title: 📖 Overview — When and How Testimony Falls Apart
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The Halakhah probes when perjured witnesses are disqualified — at the moment of being exposed, or only by court ruling? R' Ze'ira holds it is automatic. R' Yose distinguishes between one testimony with one warning (where partial = total nullification) and two separate testimonies. Two scenarios show 'sequential' witnesses: a previously rejected pair can be retroactively accepted on the strength of the second pair. The new Mishnah (7:4) closes the page: one witness or self-confession on the slaughter side ⇒ כפל only.
overview.hebrewSource: אָמַר רִבִּי זְעִירָא. הָדָא אָמְרָה. עָד זוֹמֵם אֵין נִפְסָל בְּבֵית דִּין אֶלָּא מֵעַצְמוֹ נִפְסָל

slides.title: 💬 When is a Zomem Disqualified?
slides.subtitle: R' Ze'ira reads the Mishnah's rule about partial perjury.

lines.speaker: RZ
lines.text: עָד זוֹמֵם אֵין נִפְסָל בְּבֵית דִּין אֶלָּא מֵעַצְמוֹ נִפְסָל — a perjured witness disqualifies himself; the court does not need to act.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: Then how does the Mishnah's case (one of the second pair zomem ⇒ second testimony falls) work? If self-disqualification is automatic, no testimony was ever valid.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Read it as a case of warning (הַתְרָיָיה) — and bring R' Yose's brayta: one testimony + one warning = partial nullification triggers full nullification.

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: If perjury is self-executing, the moment a witness becomes disqualified is the moment of his act — not the court's verdict. This shapes how earlier testimonies he gave are treated.

slides.title: 📅 The Nisan Timeline
slides.subtitle: R' Yose's principle in action: any testimony given between act and exposure is void.

steps.content: 1️⃣ 1 Nisan: defendant allegedly steals an ox.
2️⃣ 10 Nisan: witnesses come to court and testify he stole on 1 Nisan, and slaughtered/sold on 10 Nisan.
3️⃣ 10–15 Nisan: those same witnesses testify in other cases.
4️⃣ 15 Nisan: they are הוזמו — proven to be perjurers.
5️⃣ Result: כָּל עֵדוּת שֶׁהֵעִידוּ מֵעֲשָׂרָה בְנִיסָן עַד חֲמִשָּׁה עָשָׂר בְּנִיסָן לְמַפְרֵיעָהּ הֲרֵי אֵילּוּ פְסוּלוֹת — every testimony in that 5-day window is retroactively disqualified.

callout.label: The teaching:
callout.text: Once a witness is exposed, the court treats him as having been disqualified from the moment of his perjury — not the moment of his exposure. R' Abba bar Mamal narrows this to cases where they testified together in one group.

slides.title: 🔁 Sequential Witnesses Reactivated
slides.subtitle: Two scenarios where a previously rejected testimony is accepted because of a second pair.

boxes.title: 📥 Scenario A
items: Theft witnesses arrive — court does not accept
Court tells them: we'll accept the theft testimony על ידיכם
If slaughter witnesses are zomemim → they pay על יד עידי גניבה

boxes.title: 📤 Scenario B (R' Chizkiyah)
items: Slaughter witnesses arrive first — not accepted
Court tells them: slaughter testimony is now accepted על ידיכם
If theft witnesses are zomemim → they pay על יד עידי טביחה

boxes.title: 🔗 Common Move
items: A pair's testimony can sit dormant
Liability flows backward through that activation

callout.label: Why both versions matter:
callout.text: The sugya isn't sure which pair has the activating role. Either ordering shows the same logical structure: testimony only counts once joined to a corroborating pair.

slides.title: 📜 Mishnah 7:4 — Lone Witness on Slaughter
slides.subtitle: When the second leg of the case rests on weaker evidence, ד׳ וה׳ falls away.
rows: Two witnesses (Shabbat / ע״ז / father died / הקדיש)
Two witnesses but the act has another issue

callout.label: The structural rule:
callout.text: The penalty climbs in proportion to the strength of the proof. Without two valid witnesses to the slaughter/sale, you keep the theft penalty (כפל) but lose the multiplier.

slides.title: 🐄 R' Shimon's Position on Hekdesh
slides.subtitle: When the stolen animal is consecrated, does it still trigger ד׳ וה׳?

nodes.content:
Tanna kamma:
גנב והקדיש ואחר כך טבח
⇒ כפל only

columns.label: קדשים שחייב באחריותן
columns.content:
He still bears the responsibility
⇒ ד׳ וה׳

nodes.content: R' Shimon links the penalty to the thief's continuing ownership-stake in the animal.

callout.label: The Yerushalmi's question:
callout.text: Who taught the rule that 'one penalty before court excludes another'? R' Shimon — because his version of the rule for hekdesh tracks ownership-responsibility, the same logic that limits ד׳ וה׳ here.

summary.principle: A perjured witness disqualifies himself the moment of his perjury, retroactively voiding any testimony in the gap. And the strength of testimony controls the size of the penalty: two witnesses for both legs ⇒ ד׳ וה׳; weaker proof ⇒ כפל only.

flowSteps.content:
Halakhah on Mishnah 7:3
when does a zomem fall?
Testimony is treated as a structural object: it depends on the witnesses' standing at every moment, and each piece of proof carries exactly the weight needed to support its penalty.

🎓 Key Principle:
Penalty matches proof; perjury collapses backward in time; and R' Shimon ties הקדש liability to who really owns the animal.

quiz.q: What does R' Ze'ira learn from the Mishnah about a zomem witness?
o: He is disqualified only by the court
אֵין נִפְסָל בְּבֵית דִּין אֶלָּא מֵעַצְמוֹ נִפְסָל — he disqualifies himself
quiz.e: R' Ze'ira reads the Mishnah's logic as automatic self-disqualification, not court action.

quiz.q: In R' Yose's Nisan timeline example, what testimony is retroactively disqualified?
o: Only the testimony given on 15 Nisan
All testimony given between 10 Nisan and 15 Nisan
Only testimony about the original theft
Nothing — they were valid until exposure
quiz.e: כָּל עֵדוּת שֶׁהֵעִידוּ מֵעֲשָׂרָה בְנִיסָן עַד חֲמִשָּׁה עָשָׂר בְּנִיסָן לְמַפְרֵיעָהּ הֲרֵי אֵילּוּ פְסוּלוֹת.

quiz.q: When does R' Yose's principle of 'partial = total nullification' apply?
o: בְּעֵדוּת אַחַת וּבְהַתְרָיָיה אַחַת — one testimony with one warning
quiz.e: Two separate testimonies with two separate warnings can each stand or fall on its own; one testimony with one warning falls entirely if part is disqualified.

quiz.q: In Mishnah 7:4, if theft is proven by two witnesses but slaughter only by one witness, what is the penalty?
quiz.e: מְשַׁלֵּם תַּשְׁלוּמֵי כֶפֶל וְאֵינוֹ מְשַׁלֵּם תַּשְׁלוּמֵי אַרְבָּעָה וַחֲמִשָּׁה. Theft stands; the multiplier requires two witnesses for slaughter as well.

quiz.q: What is R' Shimon's distinction regarding stolen קדשים?
o: Hekdesh always exempts from ד׳ וה׳
קֳדָשִׁים שֶׁחַיָיב בְּאַחֲרָיוּתָן — pays ד׳ וה׳; שֶׁאֵינוֹ חַיָיב — exempt
quiz.e: R' Shimon ties the penalty to ongoing ownership-responsibility (אחריות). If the thief still bears it, the animal is functionally still 'his stolen property' for ד׳ וה׳.
